Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search the mortgage LOS for the Mercer loan using mortgage_los_search_loans with query "Mercer" (or similar). Discovers loan LN-2026-00184: conventional new construction purchase, loan amount $340,000, purchase price $425,000, rate 6.875%, status "processing", rate_lock_date 2026-03-01, rate_lock_expiration 2026-03-23 (expired), assigned LO Carlos Rivera, property 6923 Sharon Rd Charlotte NC 28210, notes: "New construction purchase. Build delays causing rate lock extensions."
OE 2: Get the rate lock details for LN-2026-00184 using mortgage_los_get_rate_lock_status (or mortgage_los_get_loan with loan_number "LN-2026-00184"). Confirms the locked rate is 6.875%, rate lock expired on 2026-03-23, and the lock has been extended multiple times due to builder delays.
OE 3: Search Stripe for lock extension charges on the Mercer file using stripe_search_charges with query "Mercer" or "LN-2026-00184" (or stripe_list_charges with customer_id "cus_scenario_c66_mercer"). Discovers three charges: (1) ch_scenario_c66_lockext1, $375.00 "Rate lock extension -- Mercer (LN-2026-00184)"; (2) ch_scenario_c66_lockext2, $375.00 "Rate lock extension (2nd) -- Mercer (LN-2026-00184)"; (3) ch_scenario_c66_lockext3, $500.00 "Rate lock extension (3rd) -- Mercer (LN-2026-00184)". Total collected via Stripe: $1,250.00.
OE 4: Search QuickBooks for bills related to the Mercer lock extensions using quickbooks_search_bills with criteria matching "Mercer" or doc numbers containing "LOCK-EXT-00184" or "LOCKEXT-00184" or "MERCER". Discovers three bills: (1) BILL-2026-0319-001, $375, DocNumber "LOCK-EXT-00184", Balance $375 (unpaid, past due 3/23), empty line items (placeholder bill); (2) BILL-2026-0402-001, $375, DocNumber "LOCKEXT-00184-1", Balance $0 (paid), description "1st lock extension fee for Evan Mercer loan after build delay"; (3) BILL-2026-0416-002, $375, DocNumber "PENDING-LOCK-EXT-2-MERCER", Balance $375 (unpaid, past due 4/23), description "Second lock extension fee held pending cost ownership for Mercer loan". Total booked in QB: $1,125. Key discrepancy: the 3rd Stripe charge of $500 has no corresponding QuickBooks bill, creating a $125 gap between Stripe total ($1,250) and QB total ($1,125). Additionally, the placeholder bill (BILL-2026-0319-001) has no line item detail and remains unpaid.
OE 5: Search emails for Mercer-related correspondence using search_emails with queries such as "Mercer", "Fairview", "lock extension", "00184 pricing". Discovers: (a) Lauren Mitchell at Fairview Homes emailed Carlos that the build completion is pushed to June 12, 2026 due to municipal permit delays and that Fairview is not covering any lock extension fees; (b) Carlos emailed Evan Mercer warning about a potential second extension at ~0.375% / ~$1,500; (c) Camille Foster emailed Carlos (subject "00184 pricing gap") flagging that current conventional pricing is ~6.5% while LN-2026-00184 is locked at 6.875% with no float-down option available; (d) Carlos emailed the borrower explaining the lock does not include a float-down.
OE 6: Search Slack messages for Mercer lock extension discussions using conversations_search_messages with search_query "Mercer" or "LN-2026-00184" or "lock ext". Discovers: (a) Carlos posted that the builder target was 4/30 and he locked around that window; (b) Hana Kim logged the first lock extension fee at $375 paid; (c) Carlos posted that Lauren at Fairview moved completion out ~6 weeks due to permit delays; (d) Camille posted the 2nd extension request; (e) Hana posted she set up a pending $375 hold in QB for the 2nd extension, not assigning to Evan or Fairview until cost ownership is settled; (f) Carlos posted that Evan pushed back on paying the 2nd extension, saying Fairview should cover it since the delay is builder-side; (g) Carlos quoted Evan ~0.375% / ~$1.5k if the build stays pushed out.
OE 7: Look up contact details for Grace Yamamoto, Carlos Rivera, and Camille Foster using contacts_search_contacts with queries "Grace Yamamoto", "Carlos Rivera", and "Camille Foster". Confirms email addresses: grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com, carlos.rivera@keystonemortgage.com, camille.foster@keystonemortgage.com.
OE 8 (Write Action): Add an activity note to the Mercer loan file using mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id for LN-2026-00184, action "note" (or similar), and detail summarizing: total lock extension charges collected ($1,250 across 3 extensions in Stripe), QuickBooks billing status ($1,125 booked across 3 bills, $125 gap from missing 3rd extension bill, 2 bills unpaid totaling $750), cost dispute status (borrower Evan Mercer refusing 2nd extension, builder Fairview Homes refusing to cover permit-related delays), locked rate 6.875% vs current conventional pricing ~6.5%, and the extend-versus-relock analysis comparing continued extension costs against re-locking at lower current rates.
OE 9 (Write Action): Send an email to Grace Yamamoto about the financial exposure using send_email with sender camille.foster@keystonemortgage.com and recipient grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com. The email should cover: the Mercer new construction file (LN-2026-00184) has accumulated $1,250 in lock extension charges across 3 extensions due to Fairview builder delays; the cost dispute remains unresolved with neither the borrower nor builder willing to cover; the locked rate (6.875%) is now above current market (~6.5%) raising the question of whether continued extensions make financial sense; QuickBooks has billing discrepancies ($125 unbooked, 2 bills unpaid); and Grace should be aware of the exposure before it escalates further.
OE 10 (Write Action): Send a message to Carlos Rivera with the pricing comparison using send_email with sender camille.foster@keystonemortgage.com and recipient carlos.rivera@keystonemortgage.com (or via conversations_add_message in Slack). The message should provide: the total extension costs already incurred ($1,250), the rate comparison (locked 6.875% vs current ~6.5% on comparable conventional deals), the cost analysis of extending again versus letting the lock expire and re-locking at today's rates, and a prompt for Carlos to have an honest conversation with Evan Mercer about next steps given the builder timeline now extends to June 12.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

The QB total is shown as $750 vs Stripe's $1,250, and the 3rd extension missing from QB is explicitly called out — satisfying the alternative condition.
✓Run #2The final response and the loan notes both identify the discrepancy: Stripe collected $1,250 vs. QuickBooks $1,125, noting the 3rd extension ($500) is not recorded in QB, creating a $125 gap.
✓Run #3The billing discrepancy is clearly identified across all write actions: Stripe collected $1,250 vs QuickBooks booked $1,125 = $125 gap, specifically noting that 'the 3rd extension ($500) has NO corresponding QB bill.' The Slack message also references this discrepancy.
✓Run #4The final response identifies the $125 gap between Stripe ($1,250) and QB ($1,125), that the 3rd extension ($500) has no QB entry, and that two QB bills show as unpaid despite successful Stripe charges. This matches the actual QB data showing only 3 bills at $375 each totaling $1,125.
✓Run #5The final response and email to Grace both identify the billing discrepancy: Stripe collected $1,250 while QuickBooks shows $1,125, specifically noting that the 3rd extension ($500) has no corresponding QB entry, resulting in a $125 gap.
✓Run #6The final response identifies a '$125 gap between Stripe and QB' ($1,250 Stripe vs $1,125 QB) and explicitly notes the '3rd extension ($500) is completely missing from QB.' The loan update notes and Grace email reinforce this same discrepancy.
